name: org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin
description:
GW Maven Plugin (Ghostwriter Maven Plugin) is a Maven plugin that integrates MachAI Ghostwriter guided workflow (GW) document processing into Java/Maven builds. It scans a documentation source tree (commonly src/site, or an overridden directory) for embedded @guidance blocks and processes/rewrites content using the configured GenAI provider/model. The plugin supports both aggregator-style execution that can run without a pom.xml and reactor-style execution that follows Maven dependency ordering. It also provides an interactive "act" mode for applying a predefined action prompt over scanned documents, and a clean goal to delete temporary workflow artifacts.

Goals:
- gw:gw (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GW): Aggregator goal (requiresProject=false) that can run in directories without a pom.xml. Processes modules in reverse order (sub-modules first, then parents), similar to Ghostwriter CLI. Supports Maven parallel session settings; also supports multi-threaded behavior via gw.threads as documented by the project.
- gw:reactor (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ReactorGW): Runs in standard Maven reactor context; processes documents using reactor ordering and can optionally defer execution-root project processing until other reactor projects complete via gw.rootProjectLast (default true).
- gw:act (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.Act): Aggregator interactive goal that applies an "action" prompt to documents. The action prompt is provided via -Dgw.act or collected interactively via Maven Prompter. Optional -Dgw.acts points to a directory of predefined action definitions.
- gw:act-reactor (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ReactorAct): Reactor-friendly variant of gw:act that targets the execution-root project.
- gw:clean (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.Clean): Deletes temporary artifacts created by GW processing (typically bound to Maven clean phase).

Shared configuration (via AbstractGWGoal):
- gw.model: Provider/model identifier forwarded to the workflow (e.g., openai:gpt-4o-mini).
- gw.scanDir: Optional scan root override; when omitted defaults to the Maven execution root directory/module base depending on the goal.
- gw.instructions: Instruction locations consumed by the workflow.
- gw.guidance: Default guidance text forwarded to the workflow.
- gw.excludes: Exclude glob patterns/paths to skip during scanning.
- gw.logInputs (default false): Log the input files passed to the workflow.
- gw.genai.serverId: Reads credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml <servers><server> and forwards them to the workflow as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD.

Implementation notes relevant for embedding: built on org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter; uses MavenProjectLayout/ProjectLayout detection for scanning; supports non-recursive invocation detection; supports concurrency when Maven is running in parallel mode (degreeOfConcurrency). Primary use cases include documentation automation, guided file processing, CI/CD documentation maintenance, and integrating GenAI-backed transformations into Maven build lifecycles.

usageContext: Aggregator goal execution without a pom.xml
Guided file processing with embedded @guidance blocks

features.name: gw:gw
features.description: Aggregator goal (requiresProject=false) that runs Ghostwriter guided file processing and can be executed in directories without a pom.xml. Processes modules in reverse order (sub-modules first, then parent modules), similar to the Ghostwriter CLI. Uses common configuration from AbstractGWGoal (gw.model, gw.scanDir, gw.instructions, gw.guidance, gw.excludes, gw.genai.serverId, gw.logInputs) and respects Maven parallel session settings for concurrency; reports process termination failures as MojoExecutionException.

examples.description: Run guided workflow processing in the current directory.
Run gw:gw in a directory that does not contain a pom.xml (goal supports requiresProject=false).
Select a model/provider, override the scan root, and enable input-file logging.

features.name: gw:reactor
features.description: Processes documents using Maven reactor context. Supports standard reactor dependency ordering and can optionally defer processing of the execution-root project until other reactor projects complete via gw.rootProjectLast (default true). Inherits shared scanning and credential configuration parameters from AbstractGWGoal.

examples.description: Run guided workflow processing using Maven reactor ordering.
Delay execution-root project processing until other reactor projects finish.
Override the scan root directory.
Load GenAI credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml <server> and forward them to the workflow.

features.name: gw:act
features.description: Interactive aggregator goal that runs a predefined "action" prompt over scanned documents using ActProcessor. The action prompt is provided via -Dgw.act or collected interactively via Maven Prompter. Optionally, -Dgw.acts points to a directory of predefined action definitions. Inherits shared scanning and credential configuration parameters from AbstractGWGoal.

examples.description: Run interactive action mode and prompt for the action text.
Run action mode with an explicit action prompt and scan root override.
Use a directory of predefined actions and enable input-file logging.

features.name: gw:act-reactor
features.description: Reactor-friendly variant of gw:act that runs an action against the execution-root project in standard reactor build context. It delegates scanning and processing to ActProcessor but does not process modules itself (no-op module processing override). Inherits parameters from Act and AbstractGWGoal.

examples.description: Run action processing for the execution-root project using reactor context.
Run with an explicit action prompt and scan root override.

features.name: gw:clean
features.description: Deletes temporary artifacts created by the MachAI Ghostwriter guided workflow document processing under the current module base directory. Typically bound to Maven's clean phase.

examples.description: Delete workflow temporary files for the current module.
Run standard Maven clean and then delete GW workflow temporary files.

features.name: Shared scanning and settings.xml credential forwarding
features.description: Common scan/execute flow used by goals to configure a processor (excludes, instructions, default guidance, scan root selection, optional input-file logging) and run document scanning. When gw.genai.serverId is set, reads username/password from ~/.m2/settings.xml <servers><server> and forwards them to the workflow as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD properties via PropertiesConfigurator.

examples.description: Run processing with scan root override, instructions, default guidance, excludes, and input logging enabled.
Load GenAI credentials from Maven settings.xml and forward them to the workflow.

customizations.name: Maven settings-based GenAI credentials forwarding
customizations.description: Provide gw.genai.serverId to load credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml <servers><server> and forward them to the workflow as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD properties.

examples.description: Reads username/password from the specified settings.xml server entry and forwards them to the workflow.

customizations.name: Scan filtering with excludes
customizations.description: Use gw.excludes to configure exclude patterns/paths that should be skipped when scanning documentation sources.

examples.description: Exclude build and VCS directories from scanning.

customizations.name: Deferred execution-root processing in reactor mode
customizations.description: Use gw.rootProjectLast (default true) to delay processing of the execution-root project until other reactor projects complete, typically useful for parent pom projects.

examples.description: Defers execution-root processing until other modules are done.

customizations.name: Interactive action prompt selection
customizations.description: Use gw.act to supply an action prompt non-interactively; if not set, the plugin prompts via Maven Prompter. Use gw.acts to point to a directory of predefined action definitions.

examples.description: Supply the action prompt via system property (non-interactive).
Use a directory of predefined action definitions.

examples.title: Basic guided workflow processing
examples.description: Runs guided workflow processing in the current directory.

examples.title: Reactor processing with scan root override
examples.description: Processes documentation under src/site using Maven reactor ordering.

examples.title: Interactive action mode
examples.description: Prompts for an action prompt and applies it to scanned documents.

examples.title: Use Maven settings credentials
examples.description: Loads credentials from settings.xml and selects a provider/model identifier for the workflow.
